schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The "Vrutti Protsaha Loan Scheme" was launched by the Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka, and implemented by the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation. The scheme aims to promote self-employment and economic activities among minority communities and provides financial assistance to eligible individuals from minority communities to start or expand their economic activities, thereby fostering economic empowerment and self-sufficiency.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
Financial Assistance provided totals ₹1,00,000/- in two parts:

- 50% Loan Amount
- 50% Subsidy Amount
schemeContent.briefDescription: The "Vrutti Protsaha Loan Scheme" is an initiative by the Karnataka Minority Development Corporation (KMDC) to promote self-employment and economic activities among minority communities. The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible individuals from minority communities.

children.text: The "Vrutti Protsaha Loan Scheme" was launched by the Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka, and implemented by the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation. The scheme aims to promote self-employment and economic activities among minority communities and provides financial assistance to eligible individuals from minority communities to start or expand their economic activities, thereby fostering economic empowerment and self-sufficiency.
Financial Assistance provided totals ₹1,00,000/- in two parts:
50% Loan Amount
50% Subsidy Amount
Step-01:
Applicant need to visits the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation
official website
Step-02:
Click "
Apply Online
" and Enter "Mobile Number" and "Submit".
Step-03:
Enter "Aadhaar Number" Fill in the "Captcha" and Select "Next".
Step-04:
Applicant will get OTP in his/her Aadhaar Linked Mobile Number.
Step-05:
Enter "OTP" and select "Continue", After that Allow the official notice.
Step-06:
After verifying the Aadhaar again fill in the "OTP" and Submit.
Step-07:
Select the scheme "Sikhligar’s Community Development Scheme" and fill in the basic details father's name, gender, qualification detail
Step-08:
Select "Next" Fill in the address detail and again select "Next".
Step-09:
Upload the required document passport-size photo, educational document, income certificate, etc.
Step-10:
Click "Proceed" and Select "Submit Application" and get an Application ID.

applicationProcess.process_md:
**Step-01:** Applicant need to visits the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation [official website](https://kmdconline.karnataka.gov.in/Portal/home).  
**Step-02:** Click "[Apply Online](https://kmdconline.karnataka.gov.in/Portal/login)" and Enter "Mobile Number" and "Submit".   
**Step-03:** Enter "Aadhaar Number" Fill in the "Captcha" and Select "Next".
**Step-04:** Applicant will get OTP in his/her Aadhaar Linked Mobile Number.
**Step-05:** Enter "OTP" and select "Continue", After that Allow the official notice.
**Step-06:** After verifying the Aadhaar again fill in the "OTP" and Submit.
**Step-07:** Select the scheme "Sikhligar’s Community Development Scheme" and fill in the basic details father's name, gender, qualification detail
**Step-08:** Select "Next" Fill in the address detail and again select "Next".
**Step-09:** Upload the required document passport-size photo, educational document, income certificate, etc. 
**Step-10:** Click "Proceed" and Select "Submit Application" and get an Application ID.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should belong to a religious minority community as defined in the Government Order.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka.
1. The age limit should be between 18 to 55 years.
1. The annual income of the applicant's family from all sources should be less than ₹1,03,000/- in urban areas and less than ₹81,000/- in rural areas.
1. No member of the applicant's family shall be an employee of the State/Central/ Government PSU.
1. The applicant and family members should not have availed of benefits under any other scheme (Excluding the Arivu Scheme) of the KMDCL in the last 5 years.

children.text: The applicant should belong to a religious minority community as defined in the Government Order.
The applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka.
The age limit should be between 18 to 55 years.
The annual income of the applicant's family from all sources should be less than ₹1,03,000/- in urban areas and less than ₹81,000/- in rural areas.
No member of the applicant's family shall be an employee of the State/Central/ Government PSU.
The applicant and family members should not have availed of benefits under any other scheme (Excluding the Arivu Scheme) of the KMDCL in the last 5 years.
